critic_raw:
CRITIQUE:

C1 — SAFETY-NET DISTRIBUTION SHIFT: No validation data from the safety-net population. Six-month extension timeline requires an active validation plan.

C2 — THRESHOLD COST-BENEFIT GAP: Capacity-driven threshold has not been verified against the $45/$12,000 cost ratio. A break-even analysis is missing.

C3 — HRRP PENALTY NOT ANALYZED: Model performance on HRRP-specific diagnoses has not been evaluated. Institutional penalty exposure depends on this.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE (responding to critic):

C1 — The six-month gap before extension is an opportunity for validation. If the team uses this window to collect prospective safety-net data, the concern is addressed before extension. I concede that no validation plan is described — this is a gap.

C2 — Cost-benefit analysis at the current threshold is feasible and should be performed before deployment. I concede this is an actionable gap.

C3 — HRRP-stratified analysis is analytically important but can be conducted using the existing 12-month holdout data stratified by diagnosis group. This can be done pre-launch.

All three are actionable pre-deployment analysis requirements rather than reasons to reject the model.
adjudication_raw: Round 1: Defender concedes all three concerns as actionable gaps. The deployment to existing hospitals is supportable if the cost-benefit threshold analysis is conducted. Safety-net extension requires validation gate. HRRP analysis is pre-launch feasible. Result: empirical_test_agreed with three pre-specified conditions.

empirical_test.measure: Prospective validation at safety-net hospital; cost-benefit analysis at 80th percentile threshold; HRRP diagnosis-stratified holdout performance
empirical_test.success_criterion: Safety-net AUC-ROC >= 0.68; threshold precision above break-even for $45/$12,000 cost ratio; HRRP groups not systematically missed
empirical_test.failure_criterion: Safety-net performance substantially below existing hospital performance; threshold below break-even; HRRP diagnosis groups show >20% relative recall gap
